title: Origins of Vampire Mythology

jokes.text: Best friends, best friends... ♪ Making a cake ♪ - Wait. Ah. ♪ Best friends, best friends ♪ ♪ making a cake ♪ - Pyoo! I never thought I'd miss it.
jokes.setup: Troy and Abed doing their signature childish song routine
jokes.punchline: Someone's nostalgic reaction to missing their antics
jokes.explanation: Strong character integration - this is quintessentially Troy and Abed behavior that defines their dynamic

jokes.text: How come I'm not best friends with anyone in the group? Aw! Don't patronize me. Oh.
jokes.setup: Pierce expressing vulnerability about friendship
jokes.punchline: Group's immediate pity response and Pierce rejecting it
jokes.explanation: Efficient character moment - Pierce's neediness and the group's predictable response pattern

jokes.text: Just letting the students know that if you're on campus this weekend, you'll have to park on the street because of the carnival. On an unrelated note, I'm into trains now.
jokes.setup: Professional announcement about carnival parking
jokes.punchline: Random personal declaration about trains
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dean moment - his tendency to overshare random personal details in official contexts. Excellent economy and character specificity.

jokes.text: Choo choo!
jokes.setup: Dean's earlier train obsession declaration
jokes.punchline: Making train sounds mid-conversation
jokes.explanation: Good callback execution - Dean fully committing to his random train persona

jokes.text: His name is Blade and... What? Oh, no, Britta!
jokes.setup: Britta about to reveal boyfriend's name
jokes.punchline: Group's horrified reaction to just the name
jokes.explanation: Strong setup/payoff - the name alone being enough to horrify everyone

jokes.text: She invoked friendship to undercut the laugh, and we're still laughing. That's how funny it is!
jokes.setup: Britta trying to guilt-trip them about laughing
jokes.punchline: Abed analyzing the meta-comedy of the situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-comedy - Abed analyzing the comedy dynamics in real time, perfectly in character

jokes.text: His name is Blade. Is that legal? Shouldn't new line cinema be suing him?
jokes.setup: Learning someone's name is Blade
jokes.punchline: Treating it as copyright infringement
jokes.explanation: Solid pop culture reference with good absurdist logic

jokes.text: And it was a marvel comic in 1973. Well, nerd alert. Well, ex-boyfriend named Blade alert.
jokes.setup: Jeff calling Abed a nerd for comic knowledge
jokes.punchline: Abed turning it back on Jeff for knowing about Britta's boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Good comeback structure with efficient escalation pattern

jokes.text: Her love life makes Pierce seem with it. Her pain unifies us. She has the king Arthur of bad taste in men.
jokes.setup: Discussing how bad Britta's taste in men is
jokes.punchline: Three escalating comparisons ending with literary reference
jokes.explanation: Strong escalation with Jeff's characteristic mix of cruelty and literary references

jokes.text: I don't understand. Uh, Andre much? Okay, I understand. I don't. You will. What's that mean? He's hung. Oh, God!
jokes.setup: Troy not understanding the sexual implications
jokes.punchline: Pierce bluntly explaining with crude specificity
jokes.explanation: Character-driven awkwardness with Pierce being inappropriately direct

jokes.text: Just like that I'm disgusting again. I was one of the gang. It was in my hand.
jokes.setup: Pierce making crude comment and being rejected
jokes.punchline: His self-aware disappointment at losing social acceptance
jokes.explanation: Poignant character moment - Pierce's awareness of his social standing and desperation for acceptance

jokes.text: So what, Britta? You're in love with a guy who's named after a kickboxing vampire movie? A fantastic kickboxing vampire movie.
jokes.setup: Jeff mocking the name Blade again
jokes.punchline: His inability to not correct himself about the movie quality
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Jeff can't help but correct even his own insults

jokes.text: All that wasted time going to my car for mid-afternoon wardrobe adjustments.
jokes.setup: Jeff expressing happiness about having a locker
jokes.punchline: Revealing his vain habit of multiple outfit changes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff vanity moment - treating excessive outfit changes as normal necessity

jokes.text: You're gonna change your shirt? Not if it's working. It's not working. Yeah. You're right. I knew it.
jokes.setup: Jeff asking for fashion validation
jokes.punchline: Immediate brutal honesty deflating his confidence
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics - Jeff's insecurity meeting blunt feedback

jokes.text: I'll have what she's having. Hey, grow up, lady! Annie! His shirt wasn't working!
jokes.setup: Troy making When Harry Met Sally reference inappropriately
jokes.punchline: Annie's outraged response and Troy's defensive justification
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Troy's innocent inappropriateness

jokes.text: I will end up doing him like a crossword, and I will regret it.
jokes.setup: Britta explaining her weakness for Blade
jokes.punchline: Awkward sexual metaphor using crossword puzzle
jokes.explanation: Creative and uncomfortable metaphor that's very Britta-esque in its awkwardness

jokes.text: You were a pillhead, so think of Blade as Adderall. And handcuff me to the radiator like a mother-flipping carny-banging werewolf.
jokes.setup: Britta asking Annie to help with addiction comparison
jokes.punchline: Escalating to increasingly ridiculous and specific imagery
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with highly quotable, specific imagery that's both absurd and character-driven

jokes.text: Man, I wonder how many women I've affected this way. Ha ha!
jokes.setup: Witnessing Britta's desperation over a man
jokes.punchline: Troy's delusional self-assessment of his romantic impact
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Troy's innocent ego and misreading of situations

jokes.text: All aboard the human being railway! Next stop, spo-- I'll get to the point.
jokes.setup: Dean doing elaborate train conductor bit
jokes.punchline: Being cut off mid-performance by impatient visitor
jokes.explanation: Good callback with realistic deflation of Dean's enthusiasm

jokes.text: Oh, a wonderful opportunity for a young man of... Urban race.
jokes.setup: Dean trying to be politically correct about Troy
jokes.punchline: Awkward euphemism that's more offensive than direct language
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean awkwardness - trying so hard to be PC that he becomes more problematic

jokes.text: Natalie, could you get me a, um, book on how to do things? You know what, just make me a scotch and soda. Make it yourself! I don't know how.
jokes.setup: Dean asking for help with vague request
jokes.punchline: Escalating to drinking, then admitting complete helplessness
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Dean's complete incompetence and panic under pressure

jokes.text: Yeah, see that sword? It has two blades. One blade is silver, the other blade is infused with garlic.
jokes.setup: Troy explaining Blade's weapons enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: The repetition of 'Blade' given the context of Britta's ex
jokes.explanation: Subtle humor from Troy's innocent movie enthusiasm while 'Blade' triggers everyone

jokes.text: Can you check my messages for me and tell me if he called? What if my mother died?
jokes.setup: Britta asking to check phone indirectly
jokes.punchline: Immediately escalating to dramatic hypothetical
jokes.explanation: Good addiction behavior - jumping to extreme justifications

jokes.text: This is junkie talk. You just wanna know where your phone's hidden.
jokes.setup: Britta giving elaborate justification for phone access
jokes.punchline: Annie cutting through the BS with addiction expertise
jokes.explanation: Good character reversal - Annie using her past addiction experience professionally

jokes.text: That's right, Britta. It's a banana. Why is there a banana in your DVD cabinet? Read the banana, Britta. 'You are a lying junkie.'
jokes.setup: Britta finding something unexpected in DVD case
jokes.punchline: Elaborate intervention setup with message on banana
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag - the absurdity of writing an intervention message on a banana is both creative and perfectly executed

jokes.text: You're Hitlers! You're racist pedophiles! You're the opposites of Batman!
jokes.setup: Britta getting increasingly angry at being blocked from phone
jokes.punchline: Escalating to increasingly absurd insults ending with nonsensical Batman reference
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with the 'opposites of Batman' being a perfectly absurd and quotable climax

jokes.text: You don't know what that means.
jokes.setup: Britta's nonsensical 'opposites of Batman' insult
jokes.punchline: Troy's matter-of-fact observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response - Troy's innocent honesty deflating Britta's dramatic moment

jokes.text: You tell anyone we did this, I will stop letting you do things with me I'm afraid to tell anyone about.
jokes.setup: Jeff threatening Pierce about secrecy
jokes.punchline: Implication of embarrassing activities they do together
jokes.explanation: Good character moment revealing Jeff's secret shame about his Pierce friendship

jokes.text: I decided to go outside the group for a best friend. And you couldn't do better than Chang?
jokes.setup: Pierce announcing he found a best friend
jokes.punchline: Jeff's immediate dismissal of Chang as bottom-tier choice
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics with Jeff's casual cruelty toward Chang

jokes.text: Cotton candy? Russian roulette? What did you say? Cotton candy sounds great.
jokes.setup: Chang and Pierce planning activities
jokes.punchline: Chang casually suggesting deadly game then backtracking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chang moment - his casual relationship with violence and immediate innocent deflection

jokes.text: He's not as good-looking as you. I wasn't fishing for that. No, no, no. I offered it freely.
jokes.setup: Shirley commenting on Blade's looks compared to Jeff
jokes.punchline: Shirley insisting she wasn't prompted to flatter Jeff
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Shirley's maternal but flirtatious dynamic with Jeff

jokes.text: Obviously the deal is he's a dirtball, Britta hates herself, voila.
jokes.setup: Jeff analyzing Blade's appeal
jokes.punchline: Reductive psychological analysis with French flourish
jokes.explanation: Jeff's typical pseudo-intellectual dismissal with pretentious language - character perfect

jokes.text: Won't change how mustard tastes. I get it. I see the appeal. He's relaxed, he's cool. N-not as cool as you.
jokes.setup: Blade's zen philosophy about his name
jokes.punchline: Jeff's grudging respect followed by awkward reassurance to Shirley
jokes.explanation: Good moment showing Jeff being impressed despite himself, then awkwardly managing Shirley

jokes.text: You're a sister to me. Britta! Whoo! Hey, little sis, do you think you could trust me to have my phone back now?
jokes.setup: Britta declaring Annie as sister
jokes.punchline: Immediately using the relationship to manipulate for phone access
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Britta's manipulative desperation and fake emotional gestures

jokes.text: I secretly switched Blade's number in her phone to my number. So if she tries to get in touch with him, it'll come to me. That's diabolical. Yeah, you're just like Blade, man. Straight up.
jokes.setup: Annie revealing her clever phone switching plan
jokes.punchline: Troy comparing Annie's manipulation to Blade's appeal
jokes.explanation: Good ironic comparison with Troy's innocent observation about the parallel

jokes.text: And she is so codependent! And such a bad speller.
jokes.setup: Annie getting angry about Britta's behavior
jokes.punchline: Adding petty complaint about spelling to serious psychological assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Annie moment - mixing psychological insight with petty perfectionist complaints

jokes.text: Your parents named you Blade? Apparently. Well, you seem okay with it. Not much to do about it. You could change it. To what? Templeton Ferrari III? Won't change how mustard tastes.
jokes.setup: Jeff questioning Blade's unusual name
jokes.punchline: Blade's zen-like non-sequitur about mustard
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character writing - Blade's mysterious appeal captured in one perfectly nonsensical but profound-sounding line

jokes.text: Won't change how mustard tastes. I get it. I see the appeal. He's relaxed, he's cool. N-not as cool as you.
jokes.setup: Jeff being affected by Blade's philosophy
jokes.punchline: Shirley's automatic reassurance about Jeff's coolness
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the immediate effect of Blade's wisdom on Jeff's ego

jokes.text: ♪ Would you let me be your best friend ♪ ♪ I would call you every day ♪
jokes.setup: Chang and Pierce bonding as best friends
jokes.punchline: Singing an overly earnest friendship song
jokes.explanation: Character-driven cringe moment with Chang and Pierce's desperate friendship

jokes.text: I'm texting to tell you I'm not texting you any more. I'm sorry, I didn't mean that. Are you mad at me now? Fine. I guess you don't wanna know our two-year-old.
jokes.setup: Reading Britta's increasingly desperate texts
jokes.punchline: Final text claiming they have a child together
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of desperate behavior culminating in outrageous lie

jokes.text: Dean, why are you here? Ouch.
jokes.setup: Dean appearing unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: Dean's hurt reaction to the question
jokes.explanation: Good running gag with the Dean's constant presence being questioned

jokes.text: Step right up! Ding, ding, ding! Get your popcorn here. Hello? Busy, babe. Blade, just wait. Wait, Bla... I told you not to call me at work!
jokes.setup: Group creating carnival atmosphere to fool Britta
jokes.punchline: Annie improvising as dismissive Blade character
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with group improvisation and Annie's committed performance as Blade

jokes.text: She's whipped by an imaginary douche.
jokes.setup: Watching Britta respond to fake Blade's dismissal
jokes.punchline: Abed's clinical assessment of the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed observation - clinical and brutally accurate assessment of the absurd situation

jokes.text: Hey, don't knock it till you try it. I got my fake bachelor's degree, and then I cheated on the LSAT.
jokes.setup: Dean defending imaginary relationships
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of academic fraud
jokes.explanation: Dark humor revealing the Dean's questionable past while staying in character

jokes.text: You're probably wondering, how do you cheat on the LSAT? How? A magician never reveals his secrets, my friend.
jokes.setup: Chang claiming to have cheated on law school exam
jokes.punchline: Using magic show cliche to avoid explaining impossible feat
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Chang's tendency to make grandiose claims and avoid scrutiny

jokes.text: Why do we wanna know how to blow up the earth or grow a human ear on a mouse? In case we have to, Shirley. In case we have to.
jokes.setup: Shirley questioning Jeff's motives for studying Blade
jokes.punchline: Jeff's dramatic justification using extreme examples
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jeff moment - his lawyer-like rhetoric applied to absurd romantic jealousy

jokes.text: How do we keep it going? How do we know we won't get into a fight? I say we just let it happen. Don't tell me what to do! What's your problem? What's yours? I'm outta here!
jokes.setup: Chang and Pierce discussing how to maintain friendship
jokes.punchline: Immediately having the exact fight they were trying to avoid
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic structure - the fear immediately becoming reality in textbook escalation

jokes.text: You dropped over 300 bucks on my booth, so I'll tell ya. You wanna know my secret? Yes. Yes, I do.
jokes.setup: Jeff desperately wanting to know Blade's secret after spending money
jokes.punchline: Jeff's intense neediness for the answer
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jeff's desperation and willingness to pay for validation

jokes.text: You should really join the air conditioning repair program. / No. / Well, that didn't work. That's what I get for improvising.
jokes.setup: Dean's failed attempt at persuasion
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment of poor planning
jokes.explanation: Dean's self-awareness about his incompetence - character growth through failure

jokes.text: You weren't texting Blade, you were texting us. I switched Blade's number in your phone to mine. You tricked me? You tricked me!
jokes.setup: Annie revealing the phone number switch
jokes.punchline: Britta's indignant response despite her own deception
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal with both characters accusing each other of trickery

jokes.text: He's brain damaged. Well, let's not be petty. No, I'm serious. He showed me the scar.
jokes.setup: Jeff calling Blade brain damaged, seeming like an insult
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's literally true with medical evidence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - what seems like petty insult becomes genuine medical explanation for Blade's appeal

jokes.text: A loose bolt flew off a Ferris wheel and imbedded in his skull, destroying the part of his brain that feels shame.
jokes.setup: Explaining Blade's brain damage
jokes.punchline: Specific carnival accident that explains his mysterious confidence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist explanation that perfectly ties together the carnival setting and Blade's mysterious appeal

jokes.text: We can't keep going to each other until we learn to go to ourselves. Stop making our hatred of ourselves someone else's job and just stop hating ourselves.
jokes.setup: Jeff giving relationship advice to Britta
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly profound self-help wisdom from shallow Jeff
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Jeff delivering genuine wisdom while still being characteristically self-absorbed

jokes.text: Y'all ever notice that cold spot in the hallway? Oh, yeah! That thing is freezing! You know what could learn a lesson from that cold spot? The air conditioning unit in my living room.
jokes.setup: Abed doing observational comedy about building quirks
jokes.punchline: Connecting it to personal apartment problems
jokes.explanation: Standard observational comedy - competent but not particularly original or memorable

jokes.text: Y'all ever notice that cold spot in the hallway? Oh, yeah! That thing is freezing! You know what could learn a lesson from that cold spot? The air conditioning unit in my living room.
jokes.setup: Abed doing stand-up about building quirks
jokes.punchline: Troy's enthusiastic agreement leading to air conditioning comparison
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor that's more about character than actual comedy

jokes.text: I couldn't remember where I imagined the door. Then I realized I was in an actual bathroom.
jokes.setup: Abed talking about being in his imaginary room
jokes.punchline: Reality twist that he was in a real bathroom
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor about Abed's dreamatorium confusion

jokes.text: Y'all ever notice the difference between me and Troy brushing our teeth? I brush my teeth like this. Troy brushes his teeth like this. I do! I do! I brush my teeth just like that.
jokes.setup: Abed comparing their tooth brushing styles
jokes.punchline: Troy's enthusiastic confirmation of the physical comedy
jokes.explanation: Character-driven physical comedy showing their different personalities through mundane activity

tmdb_overview: When a carnival comes to Greendale, Annie goes to great lengths to make sure that Britta doesn't contact her carnival worker ex-boyfriend.
insight: Vampire lore deep-dive sustains 57 jokes across 38 minutes despite uneven emotional stakes.
